ToolUse.content: Perfekt! Vitest für das gesamte Projekt ist eine sehr gute Entscheidung - einheitliches Testing-Framework. Lass mich den detaillierten Implementierungsplan mit EARS-Notation erstellen:

args.file_text: # Strateturn - Implementation Plan ## Overview This document outlines the detailed implementation strategy for Strateturn, using the EARS (Easy Approach to Requirements Syntax) notation for precise requirement specification. The implementation follows a test-supported approach with hardcoded Stratego rules initially, designed for later YAML configurability. ## EARS Notation Reference - **Ubiquitous**: The system shall... - **Event-driven**: When [trigger], the system shall... - **Unwanted behavior**: If [condition], then the system shall... - **State-driven**: While [state], the system shall... - **Optional**: Where [feature is enabled], the system shall... ## Phase 1: Foundation + Test Setup (Week 1-2) ### 1.1 Frontend Project Setup **REQ-F001**: The system shall use Vite as build tool with Vue 3, TypeScript, and Vitest for testing. **REQ-F002**: When the project is initialized, the system shall configure Pinia for state management with TypeScript support. **REQ-F003**: The system shall use Vue Router with URL-based state management for game shareability. **REQ-F004**: When a component is created, the system shall include corresponding Vitest unit tests. **REQ-F005**: The system shall provide a consistent UI component library with Button, Modal, LoadingSpinner, and Input components. #### Implementation Tasks: ```bash # Project initialization npm create vue@latest strateturn-frontend cd strateturn-frontend npm install pinia @pinia/testing npm install -D vitest @vue/test-utils jsdom ``` #### Test Structure: ``` src/ ├── components/ │ ├── ui/ │ │ ├── Button.vue │ │ └── __tests__/ │ │ └── Button.test.ts ├── stores/ │ ├── game.ts │ └── __tests__/ │ └── game.test.ts └── utils/ ├── game-logic.ts └── __tests__/ └── game-logic.test.ts ``` ### 1.2 Backend Project Setup **REQ-B001**: The system shall use Node.js with Express framework and Vitest for testing. **REQ-B002**: When the server starts, the system shall initialize WebSocket support for P2P signaling. **REQ-B003**: The system shall configure SQLite database with Turso for production deployment. **REQ-B004**: When an API endpoint is created, the system shall include corresponding Vitest integration tests. **REQ-B005**: If database connection fails, then the system shall log error and gracefully shutdown. #### Implementation Tasks: ```bash # Backend initialization mkdir strateturn-backend cd strateturn-backend npm init -y npm install express ws sqlite3 @libsql/client npm install -D vitest @types/node @types/express @types/ws ``` #### Test Structure: ``` src/ ├── routes/ │ ├── signaling.ts │ └── __tests__/ │ └── signaling.test.ts ├── services/ │ ├── room-manager.ts │ └── __tests__/ │ └── room-manager.test.ts └── utils/ ├── database.ts └── __tests__/ └── database.test.ts ``` ### 1.3 Test Infrastructure **REQ-T001**: The system shall achieve minimum 80% code coverage for all modules. **REQ-T002**: When tests are executed, the system shall run both unit and integration tests in parallel. **REQ-T003**: The system shall use test databases and mock WebSocket connections for isolated testing. **REQ-T004**: When a test fails, the system shall provide detailed error messages with stack traces. ## Phase 2: Core Game Logic (Week 3-4) ### 2.1 Game State Management **REQ-G001**: The system shall represent the game board as a 10x10 grid with coordinate system (0,0) to (9,9). **REQ-G002**: When a piece is placed, the system shall validate the position is within board boundaries and unoccupied. **REQ-G003**: The system shall maintain game state including current turn, piece positions, and captured pieces. **REQ-G004**: When game state changes, the system shall create immutable state objects for predictable updates. #### Implementation: ```typescript interface GameState { board: (Piece | null)[][]; currentPlayer: 'red' | 'blue'; turn: number; capturedPieces: { red: Piece[]; blue: Piece[] }; gamePhase: 'setup' | 'playing' | 'finished'; winner?: 'red' | 'blue' | 'draw'; } interface Piece { id: string; rank: number; name: string; player: 'red' | 'blue'; movement: number | 'unlimited'; isRevealed: boolean; } ``` ### 2.2 Movement System **REQ-M001**: The system shall allow pieces with movement value 1 to move to adjacent cells (up, down, left, right). **REQ-M002**: When a piece has unlimited movement, the system shall allow movement in straight lines until blocked. **REQ-M003**: The system shall prevent pieces from moving through other pieces or obstacles. **REQ-M004**: If a piece has movement value 0, then the system shall prevent any movement for that piece. **REQ-M005**: When validating moves, the system shall check piece-specific movement rules before allowing the move. #### Implementation: ```typescript class MovementValidator { canMove(piece: Piece, from: Position, to: Position, board: GameBoard): boolean; getValidMoves(piece: Piece, position: Position, board: GameBoard): Position[]; isPathClear(from: Position, to: Position, board: GameBoard): boolean; } ``` ### 2.3 Combat System **REQ-C001**: When two pieces occupy the same position, the system shall resolve combat based on rank comparison. **REQ-C002**: The system shall remove the piece with lower rank, while the higher rank piece survives. **REQ-C003**: If both pieces have equal rank, then the system shall remove both pieces from the board. **REQ-C004**: When a Spy (rank 1) attacks a Marshal (rank 10), the system shall remove the Marshal (special rule). **REQ-C005**: When any piece attacks a Bomb (rank 0), the system shall remove the attacking piece, except for Miners. **REQ-C006**: When a Miner attacks a Bomb, the system shall remove the Bomb and keep the Miner. **REQ-C007**: When any piece attacks the Flag (rank -1), the system shall end the game with victory for the attacking player. #### Implementation: ```typescript interface CombatResult { winner: Piece | null; loser: Piece | null; bothDestroyed: boolean; gameEnded: boolean; } class CombatResolver { resolveCombat(attacker: Piece, defender: Piece): CombatResult; private applySpecialRules(attacker: Piece, defender: Piece): CombatResult | null; } ``` ### 2.4 Game Rules Engine **REQ-R001**: The system shall implement hardcoded Stratego rules with architecture designed for future YAML configuration. **REQ-R002**: When the game engine is initialized, the system shall load piece definitions and combat rules. **REQ-R003**: The system shall validate all game actions against current rules before execution. **REQ-R004**: When implementing rules, the system shall use interfaces that can later accept configuration objects. #### Implementation: ```typescript interface GameRules { getPieceDefinitions(): PieceDefinition[]; getCombatRules(): CombatRule[]; getBoardConfiguration(): BoardConfig; validateMove(move: Move, gameState: GameState): ValidationResult; } // Hardcoded implementation, designed for future config injection class StrategoRules implements GameRules { // Later: constructor(config: YAMLConfig) constructor() { // Hardcoded Stratego rules } } ``` ## Phase 3: Git-based P2P Synchronization (Week 5-6) ### 3.1 Git Repository Management **REQ-GIT001**: The system shall initialize a Git repository in IndexedDB for each game session. **REQ-GIT002**: When a move is made, the system shall create a Git commit with the new game state. **REQ-GIT003**: The system shall use isomorphic-git library for all Git operations in the browser. **REQ-GIT004**: When creating commits, the system shall include move metadata (player, timestamp, move description). **REQ-GIT005**: If Git operations fail, then the system shall log errors and attempt recovery. #### Implementation: ```typescript class GitGameRepository { async initRepository(gameId: string): Promise; async commitMove(gameState: GameState, move: Move): Promise; async getCommitHistory(): Promise; async resetToCommit(commitHash: string): Promise; } interface CommitInfo { hash: string; message: string; timestamp: number; author: string; gameState: GameState; } ``` ### 3.2 P2P Commit Synchronization **REQ-P2P001**: When a player makes a move, the system shall send the Git commit to the peer via WebRTC. **REQ-P2P002**: When receiving a commit, the system shall validate the commit hash and game state. **REQ-P2P003**: The system shall perform fast-forward merges only, maintaining linear commit history. **REQ-P2P004**: If commit validation fails, then the system shall request full state synchronization. **REQ-P2P005**: When commits are out of sync, the system shall reset both clients to the last common commit. #### Implementation: ```typescript interface P2PMessage { type: 'git_push' | 'sync_request' | 'sync_response' | 'reconnect'; commitHash?: string; commitObject?: CommitObject; gameState?: GameState; lastKnownHash?: string; } class P2PGameSync { async sendMove(move: Move, commitHash: string): Promise; async receiveCommit(message: P2PMessage): Promise; async requestSync(lastKnownHash: string): Promise; async handleSyncConflict(): Promise; } ``` ### 3.3 Conflict Resolution **REQ-CR001**: When hash mismatches are detected, the system shall initiate conflict resolution protocol. **REQ-CR002**: The system shall identify the last common commit between both clients. **REQ-CR003**: When conflicts cannot be resolved automatically, the system shall reset both clients to common state. **REQ-CR004**: If connection is lost during sync, then the system shall cache commits for later synchronization. #### Implementation: ```typescript class ConflictResolver { async detectConflict(localHash: string, remoteHash: string): Promise; async findCommonCommit(localHistory: string[], remoteHistory: string[]): Promise; async resetToCommonState(commitHash: string): Promise; async replayCommits(commits: CommitObject[]): Promise; } ``` ## Phase 4: WebRTC Connection Management (Week 7-8) ### 4.1 Signaling Server **REQ-SIG001**: The system shall provide WebSocket endpoints for WebRTC signaling. **REQ-SIG002**: When a room is created, the system shall generate a unique room code with 30-second TTL. **REQ-SIG003**: The system shall facilitate offer/answer exchange between two clients only. **REQ-SIG004**: When signaling is complete, the system shall close the server connection. **REQ-SIG005**: If room capacity exceeds 2 players, then the system shall reject additional connections. #### Implementation: ```typescript interface SignalingMessage { type: 'create_room' | 'join_room' | 'offer' | 'answer' | 'ice_candidate'; roomId?: string; gameConfig?: GameConfig; sdp?: string; candidate?: RTCIceCandidate; } class SignalingServer { createRoom(gameConfig: GameConfig): Promise; joinRoom(roomId: string): Promise; handleOffer(roomId: string, offer: RTCSessionDescription): Promise; handleAnswer(roomId: string, answer: RTCSessionDescription): Promise; } ``` ### 4.2 WebRTC Connection **REQ-WEB001**: The system shall use simple-peer library for WebRTC connection management. **REQ-WEB002**: When connection is established, the system shall verify both clients have the same game configuration. **REQ-WEB003**: The system shall handle connection failures with automatic reconnection attempts. **REQ-WEB004**: When data is received, the system shall validate message format before processing. **REQ-WEB005**: If connection is lost, then the system shall display reconnection UI and cache game state. #### Implementation: ```typescript class WebRTCManager { async createConnection(isInitiator: boolean): Promise; async sendMessage(message: P2PMessage): Promise; onMessage(callback: (message: P2PMessage) => void): void; onConnectionChange(callback: (status: ConnectionStatus) => void): void; async reconnect(): Promise; } type ConnectionStatus = 'disconnected' | 'connecting' | 'connected' | 'error'; ``` ## Phase 5: User Interface (Week 9-10) ### 5.1 Game Board Component **REQ-UI001**: The system shall display a 10x10 game board with clear cell boundaries. **REQ-UI002**: When a piece is clicked, the system shall highlight valid move destinations. **REQ-UI003**: The system shall show piece information on hover without revealing hidden pieces to opponents. **REQ-UI004**: When a move is made, the system shall animate the piece movement smoothly. **REQ-UI005**: The system shall display captured pieces in separate areas for each player. #### Implementation: ```vue ``` ### 5.2 Game Setup Interface **REQ-SETUP001**: The system shall provide piece placement interface during setup phase. **REQ-SETUP002**: When a piece is placed on an occupied cell, the system shall return the previous piece to inventory. **REQ-SETUP003**: The system shall validate that all required pieces are placed before allowing game start. **REQ-SETUP004**: The system shall show piece count remaining for each piece type. **REQ-SETUP005**: When setup is complete, the system shall notify the opponent and wait for their completion. ### 5.3 Connection Status **REQ-CONN001**: The system shall display current connection status prominently. **REQ-CONN002**: When connection is lost, the system shall show reconnection options. **REQ-CONN003**: The system shall display opponent's online status and last activity. **REQ-CONN004**: When receiving data, the system shall show network activity indicators. ## Phase 6: Integration & Testing (Week 11-12) ### 6.1 End-to-End Testing **REQ-E2E001**: The system shall support complete game sessions from setup to victory. **REQ-E2E002**: When testing P2P functionality, the system shall simulate network conditions and failures. **REQ-E2E003**: The system shall validate Git synchronization under various scenarios (reconnection, conflicts). **REQ-E2E004**: The system shall test cross-browser compatibility for WebRTC connections. ### 6.2 Performance Testing **REQ-PERF001**: The system shall load within 3 seconds on standard broadband connections. **REQ-PERF002**: When making moves, the system shall respond within 100ms for local actions. **REQ-PERF003**: The system shall handle Git repositories with up to 1000 commits without performance degradation. **REQ-PERF004**: The system shall maintain 60fps during piece animations and board interactions. ## Phase 7: YAML Configuration System (Week 13-14) ### 7.1 Configuration Architecture **REQ-YAML001**: The system shall refactor hardcoded rules to accept YAML configuration objects. **REQ-YAML002**: When loading configurations, the system shall validate YAML schema before applying rules. **REQ-YAML003**: The system shall maintain backward compatibility with existing game states. **REQ-YAML004**: Where custom configurations are used, the system shall store configuration hash in Git commits. ### 7.2 Configuration Migration **REQ-MIG001**: The system shall migrate existing hardcoded logic to configuration-driven implementation. **REQ-MIG002**: When refactoring, the system shall ensure all existing tests continue to pass. **REQ-MIG003**: The system shall provide default Stratego configuration that matches previous hardcoded behavior. **REQ-MIG004**: The system shall validate that game logic produces identical results before and after migration. ## Testing Strategy ### Unit Testing - **Coverage Target**: 90% for core game logic, 80% for UI components - **Test Framework**: Vitest for both frontend and backend - **Mocking**: Mock WebRTC connections, Git operations, and external APIs ### Integration Testing - **P2P Communication**: Test message passing between mock clients - **Git Synchronization**: Test commit creation and merging scenarios - **Database Operations**: Test with in-memory SQLite for isolation ### End-to-End Testing - **Framework**: Playwright for cross-browser testing - **Scenarios**: Complete game flows, connection failures, reconnection - **Performance**: Bundle size, load times, memory usage ### Test Data Management - **Game States**: Predefined game states for consistent testing - **Configurations**: Test configurations for edge cases - **Mock Data**: Realistic piece movements and combat scenarios ## Risk Mitigation ### Technical Risks 1. **isomorphic-git Performance**: Early prototyping and benchmarking 2. **WebRTC Browser Compatibility**: Extensive cross-browser testing 3. **IndexedDB Storage Limits**: Implement cleanup strategies 4. **P2P Synchronization Complexity**: Comprehensive conflict resolution testing ### Implementation Risks 1. **Scope Creep**: Strict adherence to hardcoded-first approach 2. **Test Maintenance**: Automated test generation where possible 3. **Performance Degradation**: Continuous performance monitoring 4. **Cross-Platform Issues**: Regular testing on multiple platforms ## Success Criteria ### Functional Requirements - [ ] Complete Stratego game playable between two browsers - [ ] Git-based move synchronization working reliably - [ ] WebRTC connection establishment and recovery - [ ] Responsive UI supporting desktop and mobile browsers ### Non-Functional Requirements - [ ] 90% test coverage for core logic - [ ] Sub-3-second initial load time - [ ] Sub-100ms local interaction response time - [ ] Support for Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge browsers ### Quality Gates - [ ] All tests passing in CI/CD pipeline - [ ] No critical security vulnerabilities - [ ] Accessibility compliance (WCAG 2.1 AA) - [ ] Performance budgets met for bundle size and runtime ## Conclusion This implementation plan provides a structured approach to building Strateturn with test-supported development and pragmatic feature prioritization. The hardcoded-first approach for game rules allows rapid MVP development while maintaining architectural flexibility for future YAML configuration.

The implementation follows a test-supported approach with hardcoded Stratego rules initially, designed for later YAML configurability. ## EARS Notation Reference - **Ubiquitous**: The system shall... - **Event-driven**: When [trigger], the system shall... - **Unwanted behavior**: If [condition], then the system shall... - **State-driven**: While [state], the system shall... - **Optional**: Where [feature is enabled], the system shall... ## Phase 1: Foundation + Test Setup (Week 1-2) ### 1.1 Frontend Project Setup **REQ-F001**: The system shall use Vite as build tool with Vue 3, TypeScript, and Vitest for testing. **REQ-F002**: When the project is initialized, the system shall configure Pinia for state management with TypeScript support. **REQ-F003**: The system shall use Vue Router with URL-based state management for game shareability. **REQ-F004**: When a component is created, the system shall include corresponding Vitest unit tests. **REQ-F005**: The system shall provide a consistent UI component library with Button, Modal, LoadingSpinner, and Input components. #### Implementation Tasks: ```bash # Project initialization npm create vue@latest strateturn-frontend cd strateturn-frontend npm install pinia @pinia/testing npm install -D vitest @vue/test-utils jsdom ``` #### Test Structure: ``` src/ ├── components/ │ ├── ui/ │ │ ├── Button.vue │ │ └── __tests__/ │ │ └── Button.test.ts ├── stores/ │ ├── game.ts │ └── __tests__/ │ └── game.test.ts └── utils/ ├── game-logic.ts └── __tests__/ └── game-logic.test.ts ``` ### 1.2 Backend Project Setup **REQ-B001**: The system shall use Node.js with Express framework and Vitest for testing. **REQ-B002**: When the server starts, the system shall initialize WebSocket support for P2P signaling. **REQ-B003**: The system shall configure SQLite database with Turso for production deployment. **REQ-B004**: When an API endpoint is created, the system shall include corresponding Vitest integration tests. **REQ-B005**: If database connection fails, then the system shall log error and gracefully shutdown. #### Implementation Tasks: ```bash # Backend initialization mkdir strateturn-backend cd strateturn-backend npm init -y npm install express ws sqlite3 @libsql/client npm install -D vitest @types/node @types/express @types/ws ``` #### Test Structure: ``` src/ ├── routes/ │ ├── signaling.ts │ └── __tests__/ │ └── signaling.test.ts ├── services/ │ ├── room-manager.ts │ └── __tests__/ │ └── room-manager.test.ts └── utils/ ├── database.ts └── __tests__/ └── database.test.ts ``` ### 1.3 Test Infrastructure **REQ-T001**: The system shall achieve minimum 80% code coverage for all modules. **REQ-T002**: When tests are executed, the system shall run both unit and integration tests in parallel. **REQ-T003**: The system shall use test databases and mock WebSocket connections for isolated testing. **REQ-T004**: When a test fails, the system shall provide detailed error messages with stack traces. ## Phase 2: Core Game Logic (Week 3-4) ### 2.1 Game State Management **REQ-G001**: The system shall represent the game board as a 10x10 grid with coordinate system (0,0) to (9,9). **REQ-G002**: When a piece is placed, the system shall validate the position is within board boundaries and unoccupied. **REQ-G003**: The system shall maintain game state including current turn, piece positions, and captured pieces. **REQ-G004**: When game state changes, the system shall create immutable state objects for predictable updates. #### Implementation: ```typescript interface GameState { board: (Piece | null)[][]; currentPlayer: 'red' | 'blue'; turn: number; capturedPieces: { red: Piece[]; blue: Piece[] }; gamePhase: 'setup' | 'playing' | 'finished'; winner?: 'red' | 'blue' | 'draw'; } interface Piece { id: string; rank: number; name: string; player: 'red' | 'blue'; movement: number | 'unlimited'; isRevealed: boolean; } ``` ### 2.2 Movement System **REQ-M001**: The system shall allow pieces with movement value 1 to move to adjacent cells (up, down, left, right). **REQ-M002**: When a piece has unlimited movement, the system shall allow movement in straight lines until blocked. **REQ-M003**: The system shall prevent pieces from moving through other pieces or obstacles. **REQ-M004**: If a piece has movement value 0, then the system shall prevent any movement for that piece. **REQ-M005**: When validating moves, the system shall check piece-specific movement rules before allowing the move. #### Implementation: ```typescript class MovementValidator { canMove(piece: Piece, from: Position, to: Position, board: GameBoard): boolean; getValidMoves(piece: Piece, position: Position, board: GameBoard): Position[]; isPathClear(from: Position, to: Position, board: GameBoard): boolean; } ``` ### 2.3 Combat System **REQ-C001**: When two pieces occupy the same position, the system shall resolve combat based on rank comparison. **REQ-C002**: The system shall remove the piece with lower rank, while the higher rank piece survives. **REQ-C003**: If both pieces have equal rank, then the system shall remove both pieces from the board. **REQ-C004**: When a Spy (rank 1) attacks a Marshal (rank 10), the system shall remove the Marshal (special rule). **REQ-C005**: When any piece attacks a Bomb (rank 0), the system shall remove the attacking piece, except for Miners. the attacking player. #### Implementation: ```typescript interface CombatResult { winner: Piece | null; loser: Piece | null; bothDestroyed: boolean; gameEnded: boolean; } class CombatResolver { resolveCombat(attacker: Piece, defender: Piece): CombatResult; private applySpecialRules(attacker: Piece, defender: Piece): CombatResult | null; } ``` ### 2.4 Game Rules Engine **REQ-R001**: The validateMove(move: Move, gameState: GameState): ValidationResult; } // Hardcoded implementation, designed for future config injection class StrategoRules implements GameRules { // Later: the peer via WebRTC. **REQ-P2P002**: When receiving a commit, the system shall validate the commit hash and game state. **REQ-P2P003**: The system shall perform fast-forward merges only, maintaining linear commit history. **REQ-P2P004**: If commit validation fails, then the system shall request full state synchronization. **REQ-P2P005**: When commits are out of sync, the system shall reset both clients to the last common commit. #### Implementation: ```typescript interface P2PMessage { type: 'git_push' | 'sync_request' | 'sync_response' | 'reconnect'; commitHash?: string; commitObject?: CommitObject; gameState?: GameState; lastKnownHash?: string; } class P2PGameSync { async sendMove(move: Move, commitHash: string): Promise; async receiveCommit(message: P2PMessage): Promise; async requestSync(lastKnownHash: string): Promise; async handleSyncConflict(): Promise; } ``` ### 3.3 Conflict Resolution **REQ-CR001**: When hash mismatches are detected, the system shall initiate conflict resolution protocol. **REQ-CR002**: The system shall identify the last common commit between both clients. **REQ-CR003**: When conflicts cannot be resolved automatically, the system shall reset both clients to common state. **REQ-CR004**: If connection is lost during sync, then the system shall cache commits for later synchronization. #### Implementation: ```typescript class ConflictResolver { async detectConflict(localHash: string, remoteHash: string): Promise; async findCommonCommit(localHistory: string[], remoteHistory: string[]): Promise; async resetToCommonState(commitHash: string): Promise; async replayCommits(commits: CommitObject[]): Promise; } ``` ## Phase 4: WebRTC Connection Management (Week 7-8) ### 4.1 Signaling Server **REQ-SIG001**: The system shall provide WebSocket endpoints for WebRTC signaling. **REQ-SIG002**: When a room is created, the system shall generate a unique room code with 30-second TTL. **REQ-SIG003**: The system shall facilitate offer/answer # Strateturn - Implementation Plan

## Overview

This document outlines the detailed implementation strategy for Strateturn, using the EARS (Easy Approach to Requirements Syntax) notation for precise requirement specification. The implementation follows a test-supported approach with hardcoded Stratego rules initially, designed for later YAML configurability.

## EARS Notation Reference

- **Ubiquitous**: The system shall...
- **Event-driven**: When [trigger], the system shall...
- **Unwanted behavior**: If [condition], then the system shall...
- **State-driven**: While [state], the system shall...
- **Optional**: Where [feature is enabled], the system shall...

## Phase 1: Foundation + Test Setup (Week 1-2)

### 1.1 Frontend Project Setup

**REQ-F001**: The system shall use Vite as build tool with Vue 3, TypeScript, and Vitest for testing.

**REQ-F002**: When the project is initialized, the system shall configure Pinia for state management with TypeScript support.

**REQ-F003**: The system shall use Vue Router with URL-based state management for game shareability.

**REQ-F004**: When a component is created, the system shall include corresponding Vitest unit tests.

**REQ-F005**: The system shall provide a consistent UI component library with Button, Modal, LoadingSpinner, and Input components.

### 1.2 Backend Project Setup

**REQ-B001**: The system shall use Node.js with Express framework and Vitest for testing.

**REQ-B002**: When the server starts, the system shall initialize WebSocket support for P2P signaling.

**REQ-B003**: The system shall configure SQLite database with Turso for production deployment.

**REQ-B004**: When an API endpoint is created, the system shall include corresponding Vitest integration tests.

**REQ-B005**: If database connection fails, then the system shall log error and gracefully shutdown.

### 1.3 Test Infrastructure

**REQ-T001**: The system shall achieve minimum 80% code coverage for all modules.

**REQ-T002**: When tests are executed, the system shall run both unit and integration tests in parallel.

**REQ-T003**: The system shall use test databases and mock WebSocket connections for isolated testing.

**REQ-T004**: When a test fails, the system shall provide detailed error messages with stack traces.

## Phase 2: Core Game Logic (Week 3-4)

### 2.1 Game State Management

**REQ-G001**: The system shall represent the game board as a 10x10 grid with coordinate system (0,0) to (9,9).

**REQ-G002**: When a piece is placed, the system shall validate the position is within board boundaries and unoccupied.

**REQ-G003**: The system shall maintain game state including current turn, piece positions, and captured pieces.

**REQ-G004**: When game state changes, the system shall create immutable state objects for predictable updates.

### 2.2 Movement System

**REQ-M001**: The system shall allow pieces with movement value 1 to move to adjacent cells (up, down, left, right).

**REQ-M002**: When a piece has unlimited movement, the system shall allow movement in straight lines until blocked.

**REQ-M003**: The system shall prevent pieces from moving through other pieces or obstacles.

**REQ-M004**: If a piece has movement value 0, then the system shall prevent any movement for that piece.

**REQ-M005**: When validating moves, the system shall check piece-specific movement rules before allowing the move.

### 2.3 Combat System

**REQ-C001**: When two pieces occupy the same position, the system shall resolve combat based on rank comparison.

**REQ-C002**: The system shall remove the piece with lower rank, while the higher rank piece survives.

**REQ-C003**: If both pieces have equal rank, then the system shall remove both pieces from the board.

**REQ-C004**: When a Spy (rank 1) attacks a Marshal (rank 10), the system shall remove the Marshal (special rule).

**REQ-C005**: When any piece attacks a Bomb (rank 0), the system shall remove the attacking piece, except for Miners.

**REQ-C006**: When a Miner attacks a Bomb, the system shall remove the Bomb and keep the Miner.

**REQ-C007**: When any piece attacks the Flag (rank -1), the system shall end the game with victory for the attacking player.

## Phase 3: Git-based P2P Synchronization (Week 5-6)

### 3.1 Git Repository Management

**REQ-GIT001**: The system shall initialize a Git repository in IndexedDB for each game session.

**REQ-GIT002**: When a move is made, the system shall create a Git commit with the new game state.

**REQ-GIT003**: The system shall use isomorphic-git library for all Git operations in the browser.

**REQ-GIT004**: When creating commits, the system shall include move metadata (player, timestamp, move description).

**REQ-GIT005**: If Git operations fail, then the system shall log errors and attempt recovery.

### 3.2 P2P Commit Synchronization

**REQ-P2P001**: When a player makes a move, the system shall send the Git commit to the peer via WebRTC.

**REQ-P2P002**: When receiving a commit, the system shall validate the commit hash and game state.

**REQ-P2P003**: The system shall perform fast-forward merges only, maintaining linear commit history.

**REQ-P2P004**: If commit validation fails, then the system shall request full state synchronization.

**REQ-P2P005**: When commits are out of sync, the system shall reset both clients to the last common commit.

## Phase 4: WebRTC Connection Management (Week 7-8)

### 4.1 Signaling Server

**REQ-SIG001**: The system shall provide WebSocket endpoints for WebRTC signaling.

**REQ-SIG002**: When a room is created, the system shall generate a unique room code with 30-second TTL.

**REQ-SIG003**: The system shall facilitate offer/answer exchange between two clients only.

**REQ-SIG004**: When signaling is complete, the system shall close the server connection.

**REQ-SIG005**: If room capacity exceeds 2 players, then the system shall reject additional connections.

### 4.2 WebRTC Connection

**REQ-WEB001**: The system shall use simple-peer library for WebRTC connection management.

**REQ-WEB002**: When connection is established, the system shall verify both clients have the same game configuration.

**REQ-WEB003**: The system shall handle connection failures with automatic reconnection attempts.

**REQ-WEB004**: When data is received, the system shall validate message format before processing.

**REQ-WEB005**: If connection is lost, then the system shall display reconnection UI and cache game state.

## Phase 5: User Interface (Week 9-10)

### 5.1 Game Board Component

**REQ-UI001**: The system shall display a 10x10 game board with clear cell boundaries.

**REQ-UI002**: When a piece is clicked, the system shall highlight valid move destinations.

**REQ-UI003**: The system shall show piece information on hover without revealing hidden pieces to opponents.

**REQ-UI004**: When a move is made, the system shall animate the piece movement smoothly.

**REQ-UI005**: The system shall display captured pieces in separate areas for each player.

### 5.2 Game Setup Interface

**REQ-SETUP001**: The system shall provide piece placement interface during setup phase.

**REQ-SETUP002**: When a piece is placed on an occupied cell, the system shall return the previous piece to inventory.

**REQ-SETUP003**: The system shall validate that all required pieces are placed before allowing game start.

**REQ-SETUP004**: The system shall show piece count remaining for each piece type.

**REQ-SETUP005**: When setup is complete, the system shall notify the opponent and wait for their completion.

### 5.3 Connection Status

**REQ-CONN001**: The system shall display current connection status prominently.

**REQ-CONN002**: When connection is lost, the system shall show reconnection options.

**REQ-CONN003**: The system shall display opponent's online status and last activity.

**REQ-CONN004**: When receiving data, the system shall show network activity indicators.

## Phase 6: Integration & Testing (Week 11-12)

### 6.1 End-to-End Testing

**REQ-E2E001**: The system shall support complete game sessions from setup to victory.

**REQ-E2E002**: When testing P2P functionality, the system shall simulate network conditions and failures.

**REQ-E2E003**: The system shall validate Git synchronization under various scenarios (reconnection, conflicts).

**REQ-E2E004**: The system shall test cross-browser compatibility for WebRTC connections.

### 6.2 Performance Testing

**REQ-PERF001**: The system shall load within 3 seconds on standard broadband connections.

**REQ-PERF002**: When making moves, the system shall respond within 100ms for local actions.

**REQ-PERF003**: The system shall handle Git repositories with up to 1000 commits without performance degradation.

**REQ-PERF004**: The system shall maintain 60fps during piece animations and board interactions.

## Phase 7: YAML Configuration System (Week 13-14)

### 7.1 Configuration Architecture

**REQ-YAML001**: The system shall refactor hardcoded rules to accept YAML configuration objects.

**REQ-YAML002**: When loading configurations, the system shall validate YAML schema before applying rules.

**REQ-YAML003**: The system shall maintain backward compatibility with existing game states.

**REQ-YAML004**: Where custom configurations are used, the system shall store configuration hash in Git commits.

### 7.2 Configuration Migration

**REQ-MIG001**: The system shall migrate existing hardcoded logic to configuration-driven implementation.

**REQ-MIG002**: When refactoring, the system shall ensure all existing tests continue to pass.

**REQ-MIG003**: The system shall provide default Stratego configuration that matches previous hardcoded behavior.

**REQ-MIG004**: The system shall validate that game logic produces identical results before and after migration.

## Testing Strategy

### Unit Testing (Vitest)
- **Coverage Target**: 90% for core game logic, 80% for UI components
- **Frontend**: Stores, Composables, Utils, Vue Components
- **Backend**: Game Logic, WebSocket Handlers, API Endpoints
- **Mocking**: WebRTC connections, Git operations, external APIs

### Integration Testing (Vitest)
- **P2P Communication**: Message passing between mock clients
- **Git Synchronization**: Commit creation and merging scenarios
- **Database Operations**: In-memory SQLite for isolation

### End-to-End Testing (Playwright)
- **Cross-Browser**: Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge compatibility
- **Game Flows**: Complete sessions from setup to victory
- **Network Scenarios**: Connection failures, reconnection, latency

## Risk Mitigation

### Technical Risks
1. **isomorphic-git Performance**: Early prototyping and benchmarking
2. **WebRTC Browser Compatibility**: Extensive cross-browser testing
3. **IndexedDB Storage Limits**: Implement cleanup strategies
4. **P2P Synchronization Complexity**: Comprehensive conflict resolution testing

### Implementation Risks
1. **Scope Creep**: Strict adherence to hardcoded-first approach
2. **Test Maintenance**: Automated test generation where possible
3. **Performance Degradation**: Continuous performance monitoring
4. **Cross-Platform Issues**: Regular testing on multiple platforms

## Success Criteria

### Functional Requirements
- [ ] Complete Stratego game playable between two browsers
- [ ] Git-based move synchronization working reliably
- [ ] WebRTC connection establishment and recovery
- [ ] Responsive UI supporting desktop and mobile browsers

### Non-Functional Requirements
- [ ] 90% test coverage for core logic
- [ ] Sub-3-second initial load time
- [ ] Sub-100ms local interaction response time
- [ ] Support for Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge browsers

### Quality Gates
- [ ] All tests passing in CI/CD pipeline
- [ ] No critical security vulnerabilities
- [ ] Accessibility compliance (WCAG 2.1 AA)
- [ ] Performance budgets met for bundle size and runtime
args.summary: Create comprehensive implementation plan with EARS notation

orig_args.file_text:
# Strateturn - Implementation Plan

## Overview

This document outlines the detailed implementation strategy for Strateturn, using the EARS (Easy Approach to Requirements Syntax) notation for precise requirement specification. The implementation follows a test-supported approach with hardcoded Stratego rules initially, designed for later YAML configurability.

## EARS Notation Reference

- **Ubiquitous**: The system shall...
- **Event-driven**: When [trigger], the system shall...
- **Unwanted behavior**: If [condition], then the system shall...
- **State-driven**: While [state], the system shall...
- **Optional**: Where [feature is enabled], the system shall...

## Phase 1: Foundation + Test Setup (Week 1-2)

### 1.1 Frontend Project Setup

**REQ-F001**: The system shall use Vite as build tool with Vue 3, TypeScript, and Vitest for testing.

**REQ-F002**: When the project is initialized, the system shall configure Pinia for state management with TypeScript support.

**REQ-F003**: The system shall use Vue Router with URL-based state management for game shareability.

**REQ-F004**: When a component is created, the system shall include corresponding Vitest unit tests.

**REQ-F005**: The system shall provide a consistent UI component library with Button, Modal, LoadingSpinner, and Input components.

### 1.2 Backend Project Setup

**REQ-B001**: The system shall use Node.js with Express framework and Vitest for testing.

**REQ-B002**: When the server starts, the system shall initialize WebSocket support for P2P signaling.

**REQ-B003**: The system shall configure SQLite database with Turso for production deployment.

**REQ-B004**: When an API endpoint is created, the system shall include corresponding Vitest integration tests.

**REQ-B005**: If database connection fails, then the system shall log error and gracefully shutdown.

### 1.3 Test Infrastructure

**REQ-T001**: The system shall achieve minimum 80% code coverage for all modules.

**REQ-T002**: When tests are executed, the system shall run both unit and integration tests in parallel.

**REQ-T003**: The system shall use test databases and mock WebSocket connections for isolated testing.

**REQ-T004**: When a test fails, the system shall provide detailed error messages with stack traces.

## Phase 2: Core Game Logic (Week 3-4)

### 2.1 Game State Management

**REQ-G001**: The system shall represent the game board as a 10x10 grid with coordinate system (0,0) to (9,9).

**REQ-G002**: When a piece is placed, the system shall validate the position is within board boundaries and unoccupied.

**REQ-G003**: The system shall maintain game state including current turn, piece positions, and captured pieces.

**REQ-G004**: When game state changes, the system shall create immutable state objects for predictable updates.

### 2.2 Movement System

**REQ-M001**: The system shall allow pieces with movement value 1 to move to adjacent cells (up, down, left, right).

**REQ-M002**: When a piece has unlimited movement, the system shall allow movement in straight lines until blocked.

**REQ-M003**: The system shall prevent pieces from moving through other pieces or obstacles.

**REQ-M004**: If a piece has movement value 0, then the system shall prevent any movement for that piece.

**REQ-M005**: When validating moves, the system shall check piece-specific movement rules before allowing the move.

### 2.3 Combat System

**REQ-C001**: When two pieces occupy the same position, the system shall resolve combat based on rank comparison.

**REQ-C002**: The system shall remove the piece with lower rank, while the higher rank piece survives.

**REQ-C003**: If both pieces have equal rank, then the system shall remove both pieces from the board.

**REQ-C004**: When a Spy (rank 1) attacks a Marshal (rank 10), the system shall remove the Marshal (special rule).

**REQ-C005**: When any piece attacks a Bomb (rank 0), the system shall remove the attacking piece, except for Miners.

**REQ-C006**: When a Miner attacks a Bomb, the system shall remove the Bomb and keep the Miner.

**REQ-C007**: When any piece attacks the Flag (rank -1), the system shall end the game with victory for the attacking player.

## Phase 3: Git-based P2P Synchronization (Week 5-6)

### 3.1 Git Repository Management

**REQ-GIT001**: The system shall initialize a Git repository in IndexedDB for each game session.

**REQ-GIT002**: When a move is made, the system shall create a Git commit with the new game state.

**REQ-GIT003**: The system shall use isomorphic-git library for all Git operations in the browser.

**REQ-GIT004**: When creating commits, the system shall include move metadata (player, timestamp, move description).

**REQ-GIT005**: If Git operations fail, then the system shall log errors and attempt recovery.

### 3.2 P2P Commit Synchronization

**REQ-P2P001**: When a player makes a move, the system shall send the Git commit to the peer via WebRTC.

**REQ-P2P002**: When receiving a commit, the system shall validate the commit hash and game state.

**REQ-P2P003**: The system shall perform fast-forward merges only, maintaining linear commit history.

**REQ-P2P004**: If commit validation fails, then the system shall request full state synchronization.

**REQ-P2P005**: When commits are out of sync, the system shall reset both clients to the last common commit.

## Phase 4: WebRTC Connection Management (Week 7-8)

### 4.1 Signaling Server

**REQ-SIG001**: The system shall provide WebSocket endpoints for WebRTC signaling.

**REQ-SIG002**: When a room is created, the system shall generate a unique room code with 30-second TTL.

**REQ-SIG003**: The system shall facilitate offer/answer exchange between two clients only.

**REQ-SIG004**: When signaling is complete, the system shall close the server connection.

**REQ-SIG005**: If room capacity exceeds 2 players, then the system shall reject additional connections.

### 4.2 WebRTC Connection

**REQ-WEB001**: The system shall use simple-peer library for WebRTC connection management.

**REQ-WEB002**: When connection is established, the system shall verify both clients have the same game configuration.

**REQ-WEB003**: The system shall handle connection failures with automatic reconnection attempts.

**REQ-WEB004**: When data is received, the system shall validate message format before processing.

**REQ-WEB005**: If connection is lost, then the system shall display reconnection UI and cache game state.

## Phase 5: User Interface (Week 9-10)

### 5.1 Game Board Component

**REQ-UI001**: The system shall display a 10x10 game board with clear cell boundaries.

**REQ-UI002**: When a piece is clicked, the system shall highlight valid move destinations.

**REQ-UI003**: The system shall show piece information on hover without revealing hidden pieces to opponents.

**REQ-UI004**: When a move is made, the system shall animate the piece movement smoothly.

**REQ-UI005**: The system shall display captured pieces in separate areas for each player.

### 5.2 Game Setup Interface

**REQ-SETUP001**: The system shall provide piece placement interface during setup phase.

**REQ-SETUP002**: When a piece is placed on an occupied cell, the system shall return the previous piece to inventory.

**REQ-SETUP003**: The system shall validate that all required pieces are placed before allowing game start.

**REQ-SETUP004**: The system shall show piece count remaining for each piece type.

**REQ-SETUP005**: When setup is complete, the system shall notify the opponent and wait for their completion.

### 5.3 Connection Status

**REQ-CONN001**: The system shall display current connection status prominently.

**REQ-CONN002**: When connection is lost, the system shall show reconnection options.

**REQ-CONN003**: The system shall display opponent's online status and last activity.

**REQ-CONN004**: When receiving data, the system shall show network activity indicators.

## Phase 6: Integration & Testing (Week 11-12)

### 6.1 End-to-End Testing

**REQ-E2E001**: The system shall support complete game sessions from setup to victory.

**REQ-E2E002**: When testing P2P functionality, the system shall simulate network conditions and failures.

**REQ-E2E003**: The system shall validate Git synchronization under various scenarios (reconnection, conflicts).

**REQ-E2E004**: The system shall test cross-browser compatibility for WebRTC connections.

### 6.2 Performance Testing

**REQ-PERF001**: The system shall load within 3 seconds on standard broadband connections.

**REQ-PERF002**: When making moves, the system shall respond within 100ms for local actions.

**REQ-PERF003**: The system shall handle Git repositories with up to 1000 commits without performance degradation.

**REQ-PERF004**: The system shall maintain 60fps during piece animations and board interactions.

## Phase 7: YAML Configuration System (Week 13-14)

### 7.1 Configuration Architecture

**REQ-YAML001**: The system shall refactor hardcoded rules to accept YAML configuration objects.

**REQ-YAML002**: When loading configurations, the system shall validate YAML schema before applying rules.

**REQ-YAML003**: The system shall maintain backward compatibility with existing game states.

**REQ-YAML004**: Where custom configurations are used, the system shall store configuration hash in Git commits.

### 7.2 Configuration Migration

**REQ-MIG001**: The system shall migrate existing hardcoded logic to configuration-driven implementation.

**REQ-MIG002**: When refactoring, the system shall ensure all existing tests continue to pass.

**REQ-MIG003**: The system shall provide default Stratego configuration that matches previous hardcoded behavior.

**REQ-MIG004**: The system shall validate that game logic produces identical results before and after migration.

## Testing Strategy

### Unit Testing (Vitest)
- **Coverage Target**: 90% for core game logic, 80% for UI components
- **Frontend**: Stores, Composables, Utils, Vue Components
- **Backend**: Game Logic, WebSocket Handlers, API Endpoints
- **Mocking**: WebRTC connections, Git operations, external APIs

### Integration Testing (Vitest)
- **P2P Communication**: Message passing between mock clients
- **Git Synchronization**: Commit creation and merging scenarios
- **Database Operations**: In-memory SQLite for isolation

### End-to-End Testing (Playwright)
- **Cross-Browser**: Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge compatibility
- **Game Flows**: Complete sessions from setup to victory
- **Network Scenarios**: Connection failures, reconnection, latency

## Risk Mitigation

### Technical Risks
1. **isomorphic-git Performance**: Early prototyping and benchmarking
2. **WebRTC Browser Compatibility**: Extensive cross-browser testing
3. **IndexedDB Storage Limits**: Implement cleanup strategies
4. **P2P Synchronization Complexity**: Comprehensive conflict resolution testing

### Implementation Risks
1. **Scope Creep**: Strict adherence to hardcoded-first approach
2. **Test Maintenance**: Automated test generation where possible
3. **Performance Degradation**: Continuous performance monitoring
4. **Cross-Platform Issues**: Regular testing on multiple platforms

## Success Criteria

### Functional Requirements
- [ ] Complete Stratego game playable between two browsers
- [ ] Git-based move synchronization working reliably
- [ ] WebRTC connection establishment and recovery
- [ ] Responsive UI supporting desktop and mobile browsers

### Non-Functional Requirements
- [ ] 90% test coverage for core logic
- [ ] Sub-3-second initial load time
- [ ] Sub-100ms local interaction response time
- [ ] Support for Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge browsers

### Quality Gates
- [ ] All tests passing in CI/CD pipeline
- [ ] No critical security vulnerabilities
- [ ] Accessibility compliance (WCAG 2.1 AA)
- [ ] Performance budgets met for bundle size and runtime

args.new_str:
# Strateturn - Implementation Plan

## Overview

This document outlines the detailed implementation strategy for Strateturn, using the EARS (Easy Approach to Requirements Syntax) notation for business logic requirements. The implementation follows a test-supported approach with hardcoded Stratego rules initially, designed for later YAML configurability.

## EARS Notation Reference

- **Ubiquitous**: The system shall...
- **Event-driven**: When [trigger], the system shall...
- **Unwanted behavior**: If [condition], then the system shall...
- **State-driven**: While [state], the system shall...
- **Optional**: Where [feature is enabled], the system shall...

## Implementation Phases

### Phase 1: Foundation + Test Setup (Week 1-2)
- Frontend project setup (Vite, Vue 3, TypeScript, Vitest)
- Backend project setup (Node.js, Express, Vitest)
- Basic UI component library
- Test infrastructure and CI/CD pipeline

### Phase 2: Core Game Logic (Week 3-4)
Focus on business logic with EARS notation (see detailed requirements below)

### Phase 3: Git-based P2P Synchronization (Week 5-6)
Focus on P2P communication logic with EARS notation (see detailed requirements below)

### Phase 4: WebRTC Connection Management (Week 7-8)
Focus on connection management logic with EARS notation (see detailed requirements below)

### Phase 5: User Interface (Week 9-10)
Focus on user interaction logic with EARS notation (see detailed requirements below)

### Phase 6: Integration & Testing (Week 11-12)
End-to-end testing and performance validation

### Phase 7: YAML Configuration System (Week 13-14)
Migration from hardcoded to configurable rules

## Business Logic Requirements (EARS Notation)

### Game State Management

**REQ-G001**: The system shall represent the game board as a 10x10 grid with coordinate system (0,0) to (9,9).

**REQ-G002**: When a piece is placed, the system shall validate the position is within board boundaries and unoccupied.

**REQ-G003**: The system shall maintain game state including current turn, piece positions, and captured pieces.

**REQ-G004**: When game state changes, the system shall create immutable state objects for predictable updates.

**REQ-G005**: The system shall track game phases: setup, playing, and finished.

**REQ-G006**: When the game ends, the system shall determine winner as red, blue, or draw.

### Movement System

**REQ-M001**: The system shall allow pieces with movement value 1 to move to adjacent cells (up, down, left, right).

**REQ-M002**: When a piece has unlimited movement, the system shall allow movement in straight lines until blocked.

**REQ-M003**: The system shall prevent pieces from moving through other pieces or obstacles.

**REQ-M004**: If a piece has movement value 0, then the system shall prevent any movement for that piece.

**REQ-M005**: When validating moves, the system shall check piece-specific movement rules before allowing the move.

**REQ-M006**: The system shall prevent diagonal movement for all pieces.

**REQ-M007**: When a piece moves to an occupied cell, the system shall initiate combat resolution.

**REQ-M008**: The system shall prevent pieces from moving to cells occupied by friendly pieces.

### Combat System

**REQ-C001**: When two pieces occupy the same position, the system shall resolve combat based on rank comparison.

**REQ-C002**: The system shall remove the piece with lower rank, while the higher rank piece survives.

**REQ-C003**: If both pieces have equal rank, then the system shall remove both pieces from the board.

**REQ-C004**: When a Spy (rank 1) attacks a Marshal (rank 10), the system shall remove the Marshal (special rule).

**REQ-C005**: When any piece attacks a Bomb (rank 0), the system shall remove the attacking piece, except for Miners.

**REQ-C006**: When a Miner attacks a Bomb, the system shall remove the Bomb and keep the Miner.

**REQ-C007**: When any piece attacks the Flag (rank -1), the system shall end the game with victory for the attacking player.

**REQ-C008**: The system shall reveal both pieces to both players when combat occurs.

**REQ-C009**: When a piece is captured, the system shall add it to the capturing player's captured pieces collection.

### Game Setup Rules

**REQ-S001**: The system shall require each player to place exactly 40 pieces during setup phase.

**REQ-S002**: When setup begins, the system shall provide each player with the standard Stratego piece set.

**REQ-S003**: The system shall restrict piece placement to the player's own territory (first 4 rows).

**REQ-S004**: When a piece is placed on an occupied setup cell, the system shall return the previous piece to inventory.

**REQ-S005**: The system shall prevent game start until both players have placed all required pieces.

**REQ-S006**: When setup is complete, the system shall hide opponent pieces from view.

**REQ-S007**: The system shall validate that each player has placed exactly one Flag before allowing game start.

### Turn Management

**REQ-T001**: The system shall alternate turns between red and blue players.

**REQ-T002**: When a player's turn begins, the system shall enable piece selection for that player only.

**REQ-T003**: The system shall prevent players from moving during opponent's turn.

**REQ-T004**: When a move is completed, the system shall automatically switch to the opponent's turn.

**REQ-T005**: If a player has no moveable pieces, then the system shall declare the opponent as winner.

**REQ-T006**: The system shall track turn number for game history purposes.

### Git-based Synchronization Logic

**REQ-GIT001**: When a move is made, the system shall create a Git commit with the new game state.

**REQ-GIT002**: The system shall include move metadata (player, timestamp, move description) in each commit.

**REQ-GIT003**: When receiving a commit from peer, the system shall validate the commit hash matches the game state.

**REQ-GIT004**: The system shall perform fast-forward merges only, maintaining linear commit history.

**REQ-GIT005**: If commit validation fails, then the system shall request full state synchronization.

**REQ-GIT006**: When commits are out of sync, the system shall reset both clients to the last common commit.

**REQ-GIT007**: The system shall store complete game state in each commit for recovery purposes.

### P2P Communication Logic

**REQ-P2P001**: When a player makes a move, the system shall send the Git commit to the peer via WebRTC.

**REQ-P2P002**: The system shall validate message format and content before processing received data.

**REQ-P2P003**: When connection is lost, the system shall cache pending moves for later synchronization.

**REQ-P2P004**: The system shall detect and handle message ordering issues in P2P communication.

**REQ-P2P005**: If duplicate messages are received, then the system shall ignore them without processing.

### Connection Management Logic

**REQ-CONN001**: When creating a game room, the system shall generate a unique room code with 30-second TTL.

**REQ-CONN002**: The system shall allow exactly two players per game room.

**REQ-CONN003**: If room capacity exceeds 2 players, then the system shall reject additional connections.

**REQ-CONN004**: When signaling is complete, the system shall establish direct P2P connection.

**REQ-CONN005**: The system shall detect connection failures and attempt automatic reconnection.

**REQ-CONN006**: When reconnecting, the system shall synchronize game state with the peer.

### User Interface Logic

**REQ-UI001**: When a piece is clicked, the system shall highlight valid move destinations.

**REQ-UI002**: The system shall show piece information on hover without revealing hidden pieces to opponents.

**REQ-UI003**: When a move is made, the system shall animate the piece movement smoothly.

**REQ-UI004**: The system shall display captured pieces in separate areas for each player.

**REQ-UI005**: When it's a player's turn, the system shall provide clear visual indication.

**REQ-UI006**: The system shall prevent interaction with opponent's pieces.

**REQ-UI007**: When game ends, the system shall display victory/defeat message with game summary.

### Configuration Migration Logic (Phase 7)

**REQ-YAML001**: When loading configurations, the system shall validate YAML schema before applying rules.

**REQ-YAML002**: The system shall maintain backward compatibility with existing game states.

**REQ-YAML003**: Where custom configurations are used, the system shall store configuration hash in Git commits.

**REQ-YAML004**: When refactoring to YAML, the system shall ensure all existing tests continue to pass.

**REQ-YAML005**: The system shall provide default Stratego configuration that matches previous hardcoded behavior.

## Testing Strategy

### Unit Testing
- **Framework**: Vitest for both frontend and backend
- **Coverage Target**: 90% for business logic, 80% for UI components
- **Focus Areas**: Game logic, movement validation, combat resolution, state management
- **Mocking**: WebRTC connections, Git operations, external APIs

### Integration Testing
- **P2P Communication**: Message passing between mock clients
- **Git Synchronization**: Commit creation and merging scenarios
- **Database Operations**: In-memory SQLite for isolation
- **State Synchronization**: Cross-client state consistency

### End-to-End Testing
- **Framework**: Playwright for cross-browser testing
- **Scenarios**: Complete game flows, connection failures, reconnection
- **Performance**: Bundle size, load times, memory usage
- **Cross-Browser**: Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge compatibility

### Test Data Management
- **Game States**: Predefined game states for consistent testing
- **Move Sequences**: Standard opening moves and endgame scenarios
- **Edge Cases**: Invalid moves, connection failures, sync conflicts
- **Performance Tests**: Large game histories, memory usage patterns

## Risk Mitigation

### Business Logic Risks
1. **Complex Combat Rules**: Comprehensive test coverage for all special cases
2. **State Synchronization**: Extensive testing of conflict resolution scenarios
3. **Game Balance**: Validation against standard Stratego rules
4. **Edge Cases**: Thorough testing of boundary conditions

### Technical Risks
1. **Git Performance**: Benchmarking with large commit histories
2. **P2P Reliability**: Network failure simulation and recovery testing
3. **Browser Compatibility**: Cross-platform WebRTC testing
4. **Memory Management**: Long-running game session testing

## Success Criteria

### Functional Requirements
- [ ] Complete Stratego game playable between two browsers
- [ ] All standard Stratego rules implemented correctly
- [ ] Git-based move synchronization working reliably
- [ ] WebRTC connection establishment and recovery
- [ ] Responsive UI supporting desktop and mobile browsers

### Quality Requirements
- [ ] 90% test coverage for business logic
- [ ] All EARS requirements validated through tests
- [ ] No critical bugs in game rule implementation
- [ ] Consistent game state across P2P connections
- [ ] Performance within acceptable limits for target hardware
args.old_str:
# Strateturn - Implementation Plan

## Overview

This document outlines the detailed implementation strategy for Strateturn, using the EARS (Easy Approach to Requirements Syntax) notation for precise requirement specification. The implementation follows a test-supported approach with hardcoded Stratego rules initially, designed for later YAML configurability.

## EARS Notation Reference

- **Ubiquitous**: The system shall...
- **Event-driven**: When [trigger], the system shall...
- **Unwanted behavior**: If [condition], then the system shall...
- **State-driven**: While [state], the system shall...
- **Optional**: Where [feature is enabled], the system shall...

## Phase 1: Foundation + Test Setup (Week 1-2)

### 1.1 Frontend Project Setup

**REQ-F001**: The system shall use Vite as build tool with Vue 3, TypeScript, and Vitest for testing.

**REQ-F002**: When the project is initialized, the system shall configure Pinia for state management with TypeScript support.

**REQ-F003**: The system shall use Vue Router with URL-based state management for game shareability.

**REQ-F004**: When a component is created, the system shall include corresponding Vitest unit tests.

**REQ-F005**: The system shall provide a consistent UI component library with Button, Modal, LoadingSpinner, and Input components.

### 1.2 Backend Project Setup

**REQ-B001**: The system shall use Node.js with Express framework and Vitest for testing.

**REQ-B002**: When the server starts, the system shall initialize WebSocket support for P2P signaling.

**REQ-B003**: The system shall configure SQLite database with Turso for production deployment.

**REQ-B004**: When an API endpoint is created, the system shall include corresponding Vitest integration tests.

**REQ-B005**: If database connection fails, then the system shall log error and gracefully shutdown.

### 1.3 Test Infrastructure

**REQ-T001**: The system shall achieve minimum 80% code coverage for all modules.

**REQ-T002**: When tests are executed, the system shall run both unit and integration tests in parallel.

**REQ-T003**: The system shall use test databases and mock WebSocket connections for isolated testing.

**REQ-T004**: When a test fails, the system shall provide detailed error messages with stack traces.

## Phase 2: Core Game Logic (Week 3-4)

### 2.1 Game State Management

**REQ-G001**: The system shall represent the game board as a 10x10 grid with coordinate system (0,0) to (9,9).

**REQ-G002**: When a piece is placed, the system shall validate the position is within board boundaries and unoccupied.

**REQ-G003**: The system shall maintain game state including current turn, piece positions, and captured pieces.

**REQ-G004**: When game state changes, the system shall create immutable state objects for predictable updates.

### 2.2 Movement System

**REQ-M001**: The system shall allow pieces with movement value 1 to move to adjacent cells (up, down, left, right).

**REQ-M002**: When a piece has unlimited movement, the system shall allow movement in straight lines until blocked.

**REQ-M003**: The system shall prevent pieces from moving through other pieces or obstacles.

**REQ-M004**: If a piece has movement value 0, then the system shall prevent any movement for that piece.

**REQ-M005**: When validating moves, the system shall check piece-specific movement rules before allowing the move.

### 2.3 Combat System

**REQ-C001**: When two pieces occupy the same position, the system shall resolve combat based on rank comparison.

**REQ-C002**: The system shall remove the piece with lower rank, while the higher rank piece survives.

**REQ-C003**: If both pieces have equal rank, then the system shall remove both pieces from the board.

**REQ-C004**: When a Spy (rank 1) attacks a Marshal (rank 10), the system shall remove the Marshal (special rule).

**REQ-C005**: When any piece attacks a Bomb (rank 0), the system shall remove the attacking piece, except for Miners.

**REQ-C006**: When a Miner attacks a Bomb, the system shall remove the Bomb and keep the Miner.

**REQ-C007**: When any piece attacks the Flag (rank -1), the system shall end the game with victory for the attacking player.

## Phase 3: Git-based P2P Synchronization (Week 5-6)

### 3.1 Git Repository Management

**REQ-GIT001**: The system shall initialize a Git repository in IndexedDB for each game session.

**REQ-GIT002**: When a move is made, the system shall create a Git commit with the new game state.

**REQ-GIT003**: The system shall use isomorphic-git library for all Git operations in the browser.

**REQ-GIT004**: When creating commits, the system shall include move metadata (player, timestamp, move description).

**REQ-GIT005**: If Git operations fail, then the system shall log errors and attempt recovery.

### 3.2 P2P Commit Synchronization

**REQ-P2P001**: When a player makes a move, the system shall send the Git commit to the peer via WebRTC.

**REQ-P2P002**: When receiving a commit, the system shall validate the commit hash and game state.

**REQ-P2P003**: The system shall perform fast-forward merges only, maintaining linear commit history.

**REQ-P2P004**: If commit validation fails, then the system shall request full state synchronization.

**REQ-P2P005**: When commits are out of sync, the system shall reset both clients to the last common commit.

## Phase 4: WebRTC Connection Management (Week 7-8)

### 4.1 Signaling Server

**REQ-SIG001**: The system shall provide WebSocket endpoints for WebRTC signaling.

**REQ-SIG002**: When a room is created, the system shall generate a unique room code with 30-second TTL.

**REQ-SIG003**: The system shall facilitate offer/answer exchange between two clients only.

**REQ-SIG004**: When signaling is complete, the system shall close the server connection.

**REQ-SIG005**: If room capacity exceeds 2 players, then the system shall reject additional connections.

### 4.2 WebRTC Connection

**REQ-WEB001**: The system shall use simple-peer library for WebRTC connection management.

**REQ-WEB002**: When connection is established, the system shall verify both clients have the same game configuration.

**REQ-WEB003**: The system shall handle connection failures with automatic reconnection attempts.

**REQ-WEB004**: When data is received, the system shall validate message format before processing.

**REQ-WEB005**: If connection is lost, then the system shall display reconnection UI and cache game state.

## Phase 5: User Interface (Week 9-10)

### 5.1 Game Board Component

**REQ-UI001**: The system shall display a 10x10 game board with clear cell boundaries.

**REQ-UI002**: When a piece is clicked, the system shall highlight valid move destinations.

**REQ-UI003**: The system shall show piece information on hover without revealing hidden pieces to opponents.

**REQ-UI004**: When a move is made, the system shall animate the piece movement smoothly.

**REQ-UI005**: The system shall display captured pieces in separate areas for each player.

### 5.2 Game Setup Interface

**REQ-SETUP001**: The system shall provide piece placement interface during setup phase.

**REQ-SETUP002**: When a piece is placed on an occupied cell, the system shall return the previous piece to inventory.

**REQ-SETUP003**: The system shall validate that all required pieces are placed before allowing game start.

**REQ-SETUP004**: The system shall show piece count remaining for each piece type.

**REQ-SETUP005**: When setup is complete, the system shall notify the opponent and wait for their completion.

### 5.3 Connection Status

**REQ-CONN001**: The system shall display current connection status prominently.

**REQ-CONN002**: When connection is lost, the system shall show reconnection options.

**REQ-CONN003**: The system shall display opponent's online status and last activity.

**REQ-CONN004**: When receiving data, the system shall show network activity indicators.

## Phase 6: Integration & Testing (Week 11-12)

### 6.1 End-to-End Testing

**REQ-E2E001**: The system shall support complete game sessions from setup to victory.

**REQ-E2E002**: When testing P2P functionality, the system shall simulate network conditions and failures.

**REQ-E2E003**: The system shall validate Git synchronization under various scenarios (reconnection, conflicts).

**REQ-E2E004**: The system shall test cross-browser compatibility for WebRTC connections.

### 6.2 Performance Testing

**REQ-PERF001**: The system shall load within 3 seconds on standard broadband connections.

**REQ-PERF002**: When making moves, the system shall respond within 100ms for local actions.

**REQ-PERF003**: The system shall handle Git repositories with up to 1000 commits without performance degradation.

**REQ-PERF004**: The system shall maintain 60fps during piece animations and board interactions.

## Phase 7: YAML Configuration System (Week 13-14)

### 7.1 Configuration Architecture

**REQ-YAML001**: The system shall refactor hardcoded rules to accept YAML configuration objects.

**REQ-YAML002**: When loading configurations, the system shall validate YAML schema before applying rules.

**REQ-YAML003**: The system shall maintain backward compatibility with existing game states.

**REQ-YAML004**: Where custom configurations are used, the system shall store configuration hash in Git commits.

### 7.2 Configuration Migration

**REQ-MIG001**: The system shall migrate existing hardcoded logic to configuration-driven implementation.

**REQ-MIG002**: When refactoring, the system shall ensure all existing tests continue to pass.

**REQ-MIG003**: The system shall provide default Stratego configuration that matches previous hardcoded behavior.

**REQ-MIG004**: The system shall validate that game logic produces identical results before and after migration.

## Testing Strategy

### Unit Testing (Vitest)
- **Coverage Target**: 90% for core game logic, 80% for UI components
- **Frontend**: Stores, Composables, Utils, Vue Components
- **Backend**: Game Logic, WebSocket Handlers, API Endpoints
- **Mocking**: WebRTC connections, Git operations, external APIs

### Integration Testing (Vitest)
- **P2P Communication**: Message passing between mock clients
- **Git Synchronization**: Commit creation and merging scenarios
- **Database Operations**: In-memory SQLite for isolation

### End-to-End Testing (Playwright)
- **Cross-Browser**: Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge compatibility
- **Game Flows**: Complete sessions from setup to victory
- **Network Scenarios**: Connection failures, reconnection, latency

## Risk Mitigation

### Technical Risks
1. **isomorphic-git Performance**: Early prototyping and benchmarking
2. **WebRTC Browser Compatibility**: Extensive cross-browser testing
3. **IndexedDB Storage Limits**: Implement cleanup strategies
4. **P2P Synchronization Complexity**: Comprehensive conflict resolution testing

### Implementation Risks
1. **Scope Creep**: Strict adherence to hardcoded-first approach
2. **Test Maintenance**: Automated test generation where possible
3. **Performance Degradation**: Continuous performance monitoring
4. **Cross-Platform Issues**: Regular testing on multiple platforms

## Success Criteria

### Functional Requirements
- [ ] Complete Stratego game playable between two browsers
- [ ] Git-based move synchronization working reliably
- [ ] WebRTC connection establishment and recovery
- [ ] Responsive UI supporting desktop and mobile browsers

### Non-Functional Requirements
- [ ] 90% test coverage for core logic
- [ ] Sub-3-second initial load time
- [ ] Sub-100ms local interaction response time
- [ ] Support for Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge browsers

### Quality Gates
- [ ] All tests passing in CI/CD pipeline
- [ ] No critical security vulnerabilities
- [ ] Accessibility compliance (WCAG 2.1 AA)
- [ ] Performance budgets met for bundle size and runtime
